title: Best Portable Chargers for Travel (2026)
description: A short buying guide for FAA-friendly power banks with real-world use cases.
content: What to look for Our current pick Honest disclosure Flying overseas means one dead phone can ruin maps, payments, and hotel check-in. These are the criteria we use before adding anything to Outsea Picks . # What to look for Capacity : 10,000–20,000 mAh is the sweet spot for carry-on rules and daily use. Ports : USB-C PD in + out; a second USB-A port is still handy for older gear. Weight : Under 250 g feels pocketable; heavier packs are fine for backpacks only. Tip Check your airline’s watt-hour (Wh) limit before buying. Most brands print Wh on the label. # Our current pick Example 20W PD Power Bank (placeholder) Price from $29 Rating 4.6 / 5 Replace URL with your real affiliate link before publishing. View deal # Honest disclosure We test gear we actually travel with. Affiliate links help keep the site running; prices and availability change by region.
